title: cerastium arvense var. suffruticosum
intro: Cerastium arvense var. suffruticosum, also known as the field mouse-ear chickweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal effects. The plant has been found to offer various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and treating respiratory issues. Cerastium arvense var. suffruticosum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
